title: abs() casts results to xsd:integer
description:
The following query is casting xsd:double values to xsd:integer on the http://dbpedia.org/sparql endpoint (Virtuoso 7.20).
prefix dbo: <http://dbpedia.org/ontology/>

select ?a ?l (abs(?l) as ?abs) {

  ?a a dbo:Automobile; dbo:length ?l

} limit 10

Same behaviour with xsd:decimal on a private endpoint.
Very likely related to #211.
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE automobiles (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, length DOUBLE NOT NULL);
INSERT INTO automobiles (id, length) VALUES (1, 4.5), (2, -3.2), (3, NULL), (4, 0.0);
CREATE TABLE manufacturers (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR NOT NULL);
SELECT a.id, a.length, ABS(a.length) AS abs_length FROM automobiles a JOIN manufacturers m ON a.id = m.id WHERE a.length IS NOT NULL LIMIT 10;
key_bug_features: abs() function casts xsd:double to xsd:integer
